vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current visual data. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by the white rabbit anchor; the plant itself is absent or entirely obscured by soil/debris.

plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present, no plant foliage.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp, and seemingly devoid of organic life. No signs of moss or secondary seedlings.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Contains a white rabbit figurine, a spoon, and a sensor/probe in the yellow pot. No other debris.
biome_observations.anomalies: The presence of a spoon in the yellow pot and a white rabbit in the black pot suggests a non-botanical, experimental, or decorative setup rather than a functional horticultural environment.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a static environment. There is no evidence of growth, decay, or movement of plant matter, as the pots appear to have been empty or devoid of visible foliage throughout the entire 5-day observation window.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable for the registered species. The lack of any green biomass suggests either a total failure of the specimens or that the pots were never populated with the registered plants.
anomalies: White rabbit figurine in P3 (Black Pot)
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. My first step was to establish the spatial orientation of the pots and identify the anchor points. Upon validation, it is clear that the 'Expected Biome Registry' does not match the physical reality of the images. The pots are effectively empty of the specified botanical specimens. The environment appears to be a controlled, perhaps experimental, desk-based setup where the pots are currently serving as containers for non-biological items (spoon, rabbit, sensor) rather than supporting the growth of P1-P4. No plant health can be inferred as no plants are present.
